trade.summary: Trade has collapsed dramatically since 2022 due to Swiss adoption of EU sanctions. Switzerland exports pharmaceuticals and some exempt goods. Russia exports minimal goods. Pre-war trade was significantly larger. Commodity trading (energy, metals) through Geneva trading houses has sharply curtailed.
disputes: Swiss adoption of EU sanctions packages against Russia (2022-2025)

diplomatic.summary: Relations severely damaged since Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine in 2022. Switzerland abandoned its traditional neutrality to adopt EU sanctions, which Russia protested strongly. Switzerland still officially maintains diplomatic channels as a neutral state and hosts some Russia-related international talks. Russia revoked Switzerland's status as a potential peace mediator at various points.
recent_incidents: Switzerland adopted multiple EU sanctions packages against Russia (2022-2025)
Russia removed Switzerland from list of friendly nations

military.summary: No military cooperation. Switzerland refused to allow re-export of Swiss-made arms components to Ukraine (re-export ban), creating diplomatic friction with European allies. No joint exercises.
incidents: Swiss refusal to allow re-export of Swiss-made ammunition to Ukraine

financial.summary: Swiss banks froze approximately CHF 7.9 billion in Russian assets following sanctions. Swiss financial services access for Russian entities severely curtailed. Geneva commodity trading houses (Vitol, Trafigura, Glencore) significantly reduced Russia-linked trading. SWIFT exclusions affect bilateral transactions.

energy.summary: Switzerland has substantially reduced Russian energy imports following sanctions and European energy diversification. Pre-war, some Russian gas transited through Europe to Switzerland. Russian energy exports to Switzerland now minimal.

scientific.summary: Scientific cooperation largely suspended since 2022. Swiss universities and research institutions severed formal ties with Russian counterparts. Individual researcher connections remain but institutional cooperation is minimal.
